title: Network Settings.IPAddress never populates and have no connectivity to a podman container (through podman network or pod)
body:
### Issue Description

When I run a podman container from inside of a podman pod or run a podman container when attaching to a podman network the "Network Settings" section never gets populated.  This could be the reason why I am never able to successfully connect to a kafka broker running inside of a container-inside of the pod.  My containerized java application has no issues connecting to the container while running inside of the pod.  There is absolutely 0 connectivity to the kafka broker running outside of the podman pod.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pod create -n test --network <localNetwork> -p 1337:1337`
2. or `podman pod create -n test --network slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns -p 1337:1337`
3.  `podman run --rm -d --pod test <localImage>`
4. `podman inspect localImage>


### Describe the results you received

          "NetworkSettings": {
               "EndpointID": "",
               "Gateway": "",
               "IPAddress": "",
               "IPPrefixLen": 0,
               "IPv6Gateway": "",
               "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
               "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "MacAddress": "",
               "Bridge": "",
               "SandboxID": "",
               "HairpinMode": false,
               "LinkLocalIPv6Address": "",
               "LinkLocalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "Ports": {
}}

### Describe the results you expected

          "NetworkSettings": {
               "EndpointID": "",
               "Gateway": "",
               "IPAddress": "",
               "IPPrefixLen": 0,
               "IPv6Gateway": "",
               "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
               "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "MacAddress": "",
               "Bridge": "",
               "SandboxID": "",
               "HairpinMode": false,
               "LinkLocalIPv6Address": "",
               "LinkLocalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "Ports": {

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Podman version 4.2.0

### Additional information

Again, I can connect to the containerized kafka broker while running inside of the pod but have 0 connectivity from outside of that pod.

comments.body: Slirp4netns does not allow inter container communication, the ip in the container is not reachable from the outside other than forwarded ports. So you either have to communicate over forwarded ports or you must use named networks (`podman network ls`)
